description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a white ceramic body and an attached rectangular cistern at the back. The toilet features a rounded seat and lid, both of which are raised, revealing a slightly stained bowl interior. The cistern is boxy and sits directly above the bowl, with a flush lever visible on the right side. The base is broad and flat, designed for stable floor mounting, and the overall surface shows signs of wear and discoloration, especially around the bowl and seat. The design is utilitarian, typical for public or residential restrooms, and the materials suggest durability and ease of cleaning. The object lacks any visible accessibility features such as grab bars or raised seating.
description: A white ceramic floor-mounted toilet with a rectangular cistern, raised seat, and visible flush lever, showing signs of wear and staining. The design is practical and typical for urban restrooms.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open seat, bowl interior, and the cistern with a visible flush lever on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the bowl, the raised seat and lid, and the depth of the cistern.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the base, with no visible functional parts.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cistern, the raised seat and lid, and the flush lever on the left side.
